title: nie-tui crate bootstrap + workspace wiring
description:
**What:** Create the nie-tui binary crate in the workspace, add ratatui/crossterm to workspace deps, and expose nie-cli as a library so nie-tui can reuse config.rs and history.rs without duplication.

**Algorithm:**
1. Edit /home/mark/WORK/nie/Cargo.toml workspace members: add "tui" to the members array.
2. Add to workspace [dependencies]: ratatui = "0.29", crossterm = { version = "0.28", features = ["event-stream"] }, futures = "0.3", tui-input = "0.15".
3. Create tui/Cargo.toml: package name nie-tui, version 0.1.0, edition = "2021". [[bin]] with name="nie-tui", path="src/main.rs". Dependencies: nie-core, nie-wallet, nie-cli (as lib), ratatui, crossterm, futures, tui-input, clap={features=["derive"]}, tokio={features=["full"]}, serde, serde_json, tracing, tracing-subscriber, uuid={features=["serde"]}, chrono, dirs, age, rpassword, dashmap, openmls, openmls_rust_crypto, openmls_basic_credential, shlex.
4. Add [lib] section to cli/Cargo.toml: name="nie_cli_lib", path="src/lib.rs". Create cli/src/lib.rs that re-exports: pub mod config; pub mod history; pub mod profile; (and any other modules needed by nie-tui).
5. Create tui/src/main.rs with a minimal stub: fn main() { println!("nie-tui stub"); } (just enough to compile).
6. Run cargo build --workspace to verify the crate compiles.

**Defensive programming rules:**
- Never add nie-cli as a binary dep — use the [lib] path.
- Verify ratatui version matches crossterm version (ratatui 0.29 uses crossterm 0.28).
- No unwrap() calls in stub code.
- Cargo.toml must use workspace = true for deps already in workspace.

**Agent team:** Standard (R+I+V). R reads existing Cargo.toml files. I adds the crate and deps. V runs cargo build --workspace.

**Tests:** cargo build --workspace passes; cargo check -p nie-tui passes.
acceptance_criteria: cargo build --workspace exits 0; cargo check -p nie-tui exits 0; no duplicate dep warnings
